actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: An idea is practically invisible when it is first formed

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: A brand new idea is a seed, almost worthless on its own, but with great potential.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: One needs to be patient when nurturing an idea, as it will be a slow development process

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: You can pass ideas along easily, perhaps more easily than finished products.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: As your ideas develop, you need to be careful not to give them too little, or even too much, attention

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: A good idea, with care and some luck, will bear real fruit

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: An idea needs a well made bed to be its foundation before it can go anywhere.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: An idea might live forever, but it can always keep growing

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: Even the best idea can wither and die if it is neglected.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: Like seeds in a field, ideas can be stolen

actions.name: text-insert
actions.text: Ideas grow and change, but they always have a place in our minds.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: Ideas grow and change, but they always have a place in our minds.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: Ideas might seem valuable to the person who owns them, but only the grown flower appeals to outsiders

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: Scavengers might feed off of your ideas before they can bloom

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: An idea may be vulnerable and weak at first, but it could grow to be solid and hardy

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: As in the bottom of the food chain, at the basic level, all activity ultimately depends upon ideas to thrive.
